title.zh: 宇宙黎明時期的「裸露」黑洞：小紅點中的質量直接測量
title.en: A 'Naked' Black Hole at Cosmic Dawn: Direct Mass Measurement in a Little Red Dot

summary.zh:
【總結】
這項研究首次直接測量了高紅移「小紅點」(LRD)中的黑洞質量，利用引力透鏡效應與深度光譜數據，證明了這個位於紅移7.04的系統確實存在一個質量約為5千萬太陽質量的黑洞。研究發現其旋轉曲線與核星團模型不符，但與繞點質量的克卜勒旋轉完全吻合。更重要的是，這個黑洞與宿主星系的質量比高達MBH/M* > 2，是迄今發現最「裸露」的黑洞，暗示這可能是一個處於最早吸積階段的重型黑洞種子。

【研究問題】
過去的研究發現，高紅移的「小紅點」(LRD)難以用標準活動星系核(AGN)模型解釋，特別是單時期維里質量估計的準確性備受質疑。有理論認為這些質量可能被高估達兩個數量級。此外，LRDs的奇特性質使科學家懷疑它們是否真的包含吸積黑洞，或是其他天體現象。解決這個問題對於理解早期宇宙黑洞的形成與演化至關重要，因為這些天體可能揭示了宇宙黎明時期的第一代黑洞。

【研究方法】
研究團隊利用詹姆斯·韋伯太空望遠鏡(JWST)對Abell 2744−QSO1系統進行觀測，這是一個被引力透鏡放大三次的系統。他們使用NIRSpec光譜儀獲得高解析度的Hα窄線發射數據，並結合光譜天體測量技術(sub-resolution kinematics)和三維動力學建模(MOKA3D框架)分析氣體的運動學特徵。通過測量窄線的旋轉曲線，研究人員能夠區分點質量(黑洞)與擴展質量分佈(如核星團)的差異。這種獨特的組合方法允許他們在遠達70億光年的距離上直接測量黑洞質量。

【結果】
研究發現QSO1的旋轉曲線與繞點質量的克卜勒旋轉模型完美吻合，給出黑洞質量下限為log(MBH/M⊙) = 6.75 ± 0.15(約5千萬太陽質量)。更精確的三維動力學模型測得質量為log(MBH/M⊙) = 7.7 ± 0.3。這與基於寬線的維里質量估計一致，但遠低於電子散射模型預測的質量(低估近兩個數量級)。研究還發現這個黑洞與宿主星系的質量比高達MBH/M* > 2，是迄今發現最極端的「超質量黑洞」，其環境近乎原始，暗示這可能是一個直接坍縮黑洞或原始黑洞。這一發現為理解早期宇宙中黑洞的種子形成提供了關鍵線索。
summary.en:
**Summary:**
This research presents the first direct measurement of a black hole mass in a high-redshift "little red dot" (LRD), utilizing gravitational lensing combined with deep spectroscopic data. The study confirms that the system at redshift 7.04 contains a black hole of approximately 50 million solar masses. The researchers found that the rotation curve is inconsistent with a nuclear star cluster but perfectly matches Keplerian rotation around a point mass. Most remarkably, the black hole-to-stellar mass ratio exceeds MBH/M* > 2, making this the most "naked" black hole ever discovered, suggesting it may represent a massive black hole seed in its earliest accretion phase.

**Problem:**
Previous research identified that high-redshift "little red dots" (LRDs) defy standard active galactic nucleus (AGN) models, particularly questioning the accuracy of single-epoch virial mass estimates. Some theories suggested these masses might be overestimated by up to two orders of magnitude. The peculiar properties of LRDs led scientists to doubt whether they truly contain accreting black holes or represent other astrophysical phenomena. Resolving this question is crucial for understanding black hole formation and evolution in the early universe, as these objects may reveal the first generation of black holes from the cosmic dawn.

**Approach:**
The research team utilized the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) to observe the Abell 2744−QSO1 system, a triply lensed system. They obtained high-resolution Hα narrow-line emission data using the NIRSpec spectrograph and combined spectroastrometric techniques (sub-resolution kinematics) with 3D dynamical modeling (MOKA3D framework) to analyze the gas kinematics. By measuring the narrow-line rotation curve, the researchers could distinguish between point-mass (black hole) and extended mass distributions (such as nuclear star clusters). This unique combination of methods enabled a direct black hole mass measurement at a distance of 7 billion light-years.

**Results:**
The study revealed that QSO1's rotation curve perfectly matches a Keplerian rotation model around a point mass, yielding a black hole mass lower limit of log(MBH/M⊙) = 6.75 ± 0.15 (approximately 50 million solar masses). More precise 3D dynamical modeling yielded a mass of log(MBH/M⊙) = 7.7 ± 0.3. This result is consistent with virial mass estimates based on broad lines but significantly lower than masses predicted by electron scattering models (underestimating by nearly two orders of magnitude). The research also found an extreme black hole-to-stellar mass ratio of MBH/M* > 2, making this the most overmassive black hole discovered to date, in a nearly pristine environment. This discovery provides crucial clues for understanding black hole seed formation in the early universe.

keyArticle.title: A direct black-hole mass measurement in a little red dot at high redshift
